pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, README.md, and verifier.py. Multiple scripts reference this exact path.
Confirmed in export_result.sh which checks for both binary STL (80-byte header + uint32 count) and ASCII STL (solid header). README.md states 'binary STL' as expected. Verifier accepts both formats.
Confirmed in task.json metadata, README.md (line 26), and verifier.py (line 29). Multiple sources independently specify 10000 as minimum.
Confirmed in task.json metadata (204800 bytes = 200 KB), README.md line 27 'File size > 200 KB', and verifier.py line 30 (default min_size = 204800). All sources consistent.

Confirmed in setup_invesalius.sh line 73: download URL 'https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip' contains the '0051' dataset identifier. setup_task.sh references '/home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium' as the location. README.md line 38 confirms 'CT Cranium (/home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium/0051)'.

Verified through multiple web searches and GitHub sources. InVesalius 3 project file format documentation shows example threshold_range of [226, 3071]. InVesalius user guide mentions 226-3021 for bone segmentation. Web search confirms 226-3071 as InVesalius bone preset range in project file format spec.

Verified through GitHub InVesalius project file format wiki (shows example [226, 3071] for threshold_range). Web searches confirm this is the documented upper bound for bone thresholding in InVesalius 3 project files and default mask configuration.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires exporting a 3D STL model of cranial bone from a CT DICOM scan using InVesalius 3 software. The CT Cranium dataset (case ID 0051) is downloaded from the official InVesalius v3.0 GitHub release at https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip. The bone segmentation uses the Hounsfield Unit (HU) threshold range 226-3071, which is the standard Bone preset in InVesalius. The output STL file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/skull_model.stl with minimum requirements of 10,000 triangles and 200 KB file size, which for a complete skull typically produces 50,000-500,000 triangles. The task accepts both binary and ASCII STL formats, and allows alternative bone presets such as Compact Bone (Adult: 662-1988 HU) if a valid surface is generated.
